topic: destemming and its effect on tannin structure

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Stems or raspones can be present with the grapes before fermentation and can contribute green, astringent tannins.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: If stems remain during fermentation, tannins and green phenolic compounds can be extracted from them into the must.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Destemming removes this source of green tannins, leading to softer, rounder tannins and a cleaner palate structure.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Destemming improves control of tannin structure because it removes stems before fermentation; the wine therefore avoids excessive green astringency and can show softer tannins.
